idea-plugin.name: Worktree Env Configurator
idea-plugin.description:
Worktree Env Configurator — Auto-configure .env for Git Worktrees

Automatically detects when a Laravel project is opened as a Git worktree and configures the .env file by copying it from the main project and adjusting APP_URL to match the worktree folder name.

Features

  • Auto-detection — Detects Git worktree projects on open
  • Smart APP_URL — Preserves scheme, TLD, port, and path from the original URL
  • Status bar widget — Shows worktree .env status at a glance (green = configured, orange = pending)
  • Tool window — Full status panel with worktree info and actions in the right sidebar
  • Live updates — UI reacts to .env file changes in real time
  • Configurable pattern — Use {folder} placeholder for custom URL patterns
  • .env.testing support — Optionally copies and configures the testing env

Quick Start

  1. Open a Git worktree project in PhpStorm
  2. The plugin detects the worktree and shows a notification
  3. Click Configure .env or use the tool window
  4. Status bar turns green when configured

Perfect for Laravel developers using Git worktrees with Laravel Herd or Valet, where each worktree folder maps to a local hostname (e.g., myapp-feature.test).

action.description: Configure .env file for the current Git worktree
